$comment: D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY (plan 24 Stage 3) end-to-end witness: the CST->HIR expression lowerer (`lowerExpr` in src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp) is now an EXPLICIT WORK-STACK DRIVER for the deep arms (parenthesized/wrapper descent + plain-binary operands + unary operand), so a DEEPLY-nested expression lowers with FLAT O(N) host-stack cost instead of one host-recursion frame per nesting level. This program returns `-(-( (((... 200 parens ...((40 + 2))... )))) ))` = 42: 200 nested parens (just under the parser's 256 maxExpressionDepth cap) drive the paren PassThrough arm 200 levels deep, while the bottom `40 + 2` exercises the now-iterative plain-binary arm and the two leading `-` exercise the unary arm. A regression that UNWRAPPED any of those three arms back to host recursion would recurse 200 frames deep during CST->HIR lowering; before this stage the lowering ran on the 64 MiB worker stack (core/substrate/large_stack_call) and 200 levels was already past the host's ~1 MB main-stack crash point (~25 levels), so an accidental main-stack regression would crash (rc-127) here instead of exiting 42. The value 42 is miscompile-sensitive: a wrong binary combine (40 + 2), a dropped/duplicated unary negation, or a mis-ordered operand build would flip the exit code. OUTPUT-IDENTITY against the prior recursive lowerer is enforced by the full ctest suite; this example adds the deep-flatten + real-codegen run witness. The optimizedPipelines arm proves the lowered HIR survives the full release pipeline (ConstFold folds the whole constant tree to 42 in the optimized arm; the baseline arm is the real-codegen witness). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
